artists.name: Nelson P. Nelson
artists.bio: Owner of NPN Tattoo Shop. Inspired by successful temporary tattoo events at the Sumner County and South Haven fairs, he created Outlaw as a local spirit and school-unity turtle.

artists.name: Laura Ireland
artists.bio: Caldwell resident since 2016 and Oklahoma State University architecture graduate. Her work reflects family, design, art, gardening, reading, cooking, and community life.

artists.name: Jessy Wencel-Potts
artists.bio: Native of Caldwell and Caldwell Schools art teacher. Her work reflects prairie life, Kansas symbols, and the local community she returned home to serve.

artists.name: Jill Kuehny
artists.bio: Lifelong Caldwell resident, community developer, CEO of KanOkla, Finance Commissioner, Vision Caldwell director, and painter/creator for the Caldwell Art Brigade.

artists.name: Dr. Jen Risley-Kern
artists.bio: Retired Wellington USD 353 administrator, native of Caldwell, and active supporter of Caldwell arts projects and community boards.

artists.name: Sammie Strnad
artists.bio: Artist with lifelong love of drawing and painting. Her hand-painted turtle showcases attention to detail and local Caldwell identity.

artists.name: Brooke Cink
artists.bio: Owner of Creative Vibes Art Studio and What In Carnation flower shop in Caldwell. Lifelong artist, teacher, commissioned artist, and founding member of the Caldwell Art Brigade.

artists.name: Brenda Lebeda-Almond
artists.bio: Graphic design, visual communication, art education, and illustration professional connected to Caldwell since the 1870s through her family. Retired educator who continues freelance art and design work.
